description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:14 Docker image as the default image, with a cache keyed by $CI_BUILD_REF_NAME that stores node_modules/ and includes untracked files. Define stages prepare, verify, test, and publish, and a global before_script that sets VERSION from CI_COMMIT_TAG or CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME and echoes it. In the verify stage add a codestyle job that runs npm ci then npm run lint. In the test stage add three jobs named node:10, node:12 and node:14, each using the corresponding node:10, node:12, node:14 images, triggered only for tags matching the regex ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+(-rc.\d+|)$, each running npm i (or npm ci for node:14) followed by npm run unit and collecting coverage with the regex ^All files[^|]*\|[^|]*\s+([\d\.]+)/. In the publish stage add an npm job that runs manually, sets VERSION_TAG to "next" if CI_COMMIT_TAG contains "-rc" otherwise "latest", writes the NPM auth token to .npmrc, runs npm ci, npm run prepare and publishes the package with npm publish --tag $VERSION_TAG to the npm registry URL https://www.npmjs.com/package/${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.
